text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
While headed back to the academy after an
outing with Agnes Tachyon...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Trainer, have you ever had a stalker?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Because right now, I'm being stalked by someone.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Listen up. Act calm and casually look behind
you... towards the sky.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I do as she says and take a casual glance behind
me.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
A street lamp dimly flickers to life, and above it,
a single feathery figure stands...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Did you see it?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Correct, a crow. I'm being stalked by a crow.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
A few days ago, that same crow got tangled up in
some discarded fishing line.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
It was like a histone wrapped in a strand of DNA...
No, speaking in terms of conventional theory, my
description suffers from a discrepancy.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Still, the hierarchical structure of the nucleosome
has yet to be fully studied.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Oops, I seem to have gotten away from the
original topic of conversation. Anyway, I think the
crow is stalking me because I saved it.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Well, you see... Ahhh, you'll understand soon
enough.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
For now, let's make it seem as though I'm staying
in your office.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: The next day...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I go to open the curtains in my office, but as I get
closer, I notice a large heap of garbage stuck
onto the sill.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Hahaha! You see now, Trainer? It's a present from
the crow!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Its stench is strong enough that even I must pinch
my nose!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Right? It did the same thing to my room.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Without fail, it packs my windowsill with trash
until it's stuck into the window frame.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
So, Trainer... How do you think we can resolve
this situation?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: To tell you the truth, I'm at a complete loss!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
If this crow were an Umamusume, I'd ask them to
repay their thanks by becoming a test subject,
but...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
It's a crow! They're not as durable as we are! The
risk to its safety would be too great!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
So, I need your concentrated effort to get me out
of this!
choice_data_list: What if you try thanking it?
What if you try accepting the gift?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
However intelligent it may be, we're dealing with
a wild bird here, remember?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: I highly doubt words are the solution. Hmm...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Well... I suppose there is a possibility it might sense something.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Okay, I'll give it a go. You never know something
until you try it!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Hey, crow! Your gratitude is painfully clear!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
You've done more than enough! I'm incredibly
satisfied! So... you should start thinking about
yourself first now!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The crow appears to stare at Agnes Tachyon for a
moment...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text: Then it swiftly flaps out its wings and flies away.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: Oh... It seems to have worked.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
After the crow disappears, Agnes Tachyon is left
with the question of whether her words were truly
understood.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text: While I can't experiment on an actual crow, I can insert a crow's genes into you...

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
And there she goes again, spouting out some
off-kilter ideas.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
What?! Are you suggesting I eat what it's offered
me?!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
What kind of person are you?! That doesn't even
qualify as an experiment!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Uuugh... As my assistant, you should be nicer to
me!

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I only meant that she act like she'd accept it, but I
guess she misunderstood me.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
The crow continues bringing gifts for some days
to follow, but eventually it stops appearing.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
Agnes Tachyon was at first overwhelmed by the
offerings, but...

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
On occasion, there were these pretty rocks and
things mixed in... I began to enjoy what I'd find
after a while.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
She gazes out the now crow-free window and
smiles longingly.
